assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: task-1 (Repository: trustify-backend), task-3 (trustify-backend), task-4 (trustify-backend), task-8 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: task-2 (Repository: trustify-ui), task-5 (trustify-ui), task-6 (trustify-ui), task-7 (trustify-ui), task-9 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are task-3 and task-4. Frontend tasks that depend on them: task-5 (depends on Task 4 backend endpoint), task-6 (depends on Task 5), task-7 (depends on Task 6). Backend task numbers (3, 4) are lower than dependent frontend task numbers (5, 6, 7). Bookend tasks: backend create-branch is task-1, frontend create-branch is task-2; backend merge is task-8, frontend merge is task-9 — all consistent.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task-6 (SbomComparePage, UI-facing) extensively references Figma design context with specific PatternFly components: Select (single, typeahead), ExpandableSection, Badge (with colors green/red/blue/yellow), Table (composable, sortable), SeverityBadge, EmptyState with CodeBranchIcon, Dropdown, Skeleton. Task-5 (API types and hook, API-layer) is exempt and contains no Figma/PatternFly references. Task-7 (tests) references PatternFly components indirectly through test assertions (CodeBranchIcon, Critical severity highlighting) which is acceptable as testing UI behavior.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task-5 (frontend, trustify-ui) explicitly depends on 'Task 4 — Add SBOM comparison endpoint and integration tests (trustify-backend)', which is a backend task. This is a direct cross-repo dependency. Additionally, the feature-branch workflow uses TC-9003 branches in both repos with backend tasks (3, 4) having lower numbers than frontend tasks (5, 6, 7).

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks (3, 4) reference paths like modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/, tests/api/sbom.rs, common/src/error.rs — all from repo-backend.md. Frontend tasks (5, 6, 7) reference paths like src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/, src/pages/, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/routes.tsx, tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/e2e/ — all from repo-frontend.md.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have a '## Repository' section with a single repo name: tasks 1, 3, 4, 8 have 'trustify-backend'; tasks 2, 5, 6, 7, 9 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (task-1: main, task-2: main, task-3: TC-9003, task-4: TC-9003, task-5: TC-9003, task-6: TC-9003, task-7: TC-9003, task-8: main, task-9: main), '## Description' (all present), '## Acceptance Criteria' (all present with checkboxes), '## Test Requirements' (all present with checkboxes).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task paths: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ (exists in manifest as model/mod.rs, summary.rs, details.rs — comparison.rs is a new file being created, consistent with the existing directory), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/ (exists), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/ (exists with mod.rs, list.rs, get.rs — compare.rs is new), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/mod.rs (exists), tests/api/sbom.rs (exists). Frontend task paths: src/api/models.ts (exists), src/api/rest.ts (exists), src/hooks/ (exists), src/pages/ (exists with established pattern), src/routes.tsx (exists), src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (exists), tests/mocks/handlers.ts (exists), tests/mocks/fixtures/sboms.json (exists), tests/e2e/ (exists). New files follow established directory patterns. Task-5 references backend paths in its 'Backend API contracts' section but these are cross-references for context, not Files to Modify/Create entries.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks (1, 2, 8, 9) have no Implementation Notes section — exempt. Task-3 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (SbomService), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (PackageService), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs (AdvisoryService), modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs (PackageSummary with license field), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs (AdvisorySummary with severity field), common/src/error.rs (AppError). Task-4 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs, get.rs, endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, assert_eq! pattern. Task-5 references: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts. Task-6 references: src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/pages/SbomDetailPage/ pattern, src/routes.tsx, useSboms hook, useSearchParams. Task-7 references: src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.test.tsx, tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts, tests/setup.ts. All contain specific paths and code patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks: task-1 (Target Branch: main, create-branch backend), task-2 (Target Branch: main, create-branch frontend), task-8 (Target Branch: main, merge-branch backend), task-9 (Target Branch: main, merge-branch frontend). Intermediate tasks: task-3 (Target Branch: TC-9003), task-4 (Target Branch: TC-9003), task-5 (Target Branch: TC-9003), task-6 (Target Branch: TC-9003), task-7 (Target Branch: TC-9003). This matches the feature-branch pattern exactly.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 digest files follow the exact format. Examples: task-1-digest.txt: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:dfe1483ed440b60147af2165273ffff098ecd781422404fcc513bb40e946a21c' (64 hex chars), task-3-digest.txt: 'sha256:16f7df63fb077196444fd672da6c5c5c2c8d591e5097a0545bfb83ae8cf5afeb' (64 hex chars), task-6-digest.txt: 'sha256:4fdb12ed933f82f5d956e6740c864d44ce4d7065bfc179ea56d5f34b68636c55' (64 hex chars). All 9 digests are present, use the exact marker format, and contain 64 lowercase hex characters after 'sha256:'.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task output contains any convention enrichment in the prescribed format. There are no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references, no 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale strings anywhere in the outputs. While the repo manifests mention CONVENTIONS.md files exist in both repos (trustify-backend/CONVENTIONS.md and trustify-ui/CONVENTIONS.md), the task descriptions do not include any convention-aware enrichment at all. The absence of convention enrichment means the prescribed format requirement cannot be verified as satisfied.
